rulings.Case Title: JAMES SCHWERDT et al VS. BORG-WARNER CORPORATION, BY ITS SUCCESSOR IN INTER et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 22, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Mill Valley Lumber Company's motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SCHWERDT et al VS. BORG-WARNER CORPORATION, BY ITS SUCCESSOR IN INTER et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 22, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs' Exhibits J through S create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Plaintiffs' separate statement of disputed material facts, nos. 2-14.) Defendant's objections nos. 1 through 8 are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SCHWERDT et al VS. BORG-WARNER CORPORATION, BY ITS SUCCESSOR IN INTER et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 22, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s amended motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue whether decedent James Schwerdt was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
